rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports; use implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model in AGREE contracts; avoid package-qualified names not present in the model.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be syntactically valid and use only declared constants, variables, and features from the current scope.
Guarantees should relate visible outputs to visible inputs; avoid implementation-specific subcomponent paths in type-level contracts.
Constants and equations in AGREE must use valid scalar types (int, real, bool) as declared in the AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` and must follow valid AGREE syntax for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations.
Assignments in implementation scope must reference valid output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes; only use assignments for implementation-level bindings.
Do not reference non-visible or package-qualified identifiers that are not part of the AADL model in AGREE contracts.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
Model fusion and scope preservation rules
